{{Quote|And when an ultimate victor emerges from this war, I will know them better than they know themselves. This will give me power: the power to survive and thrive no matter what events may come.|translated=1|Hulas|<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''</ref>}}
'''Hulas''' was a [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Overseer (GenoHaradan)|Overseer]] of the [[GenoHaradan]] who tried to persuade the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] to join the organization during his visit to [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]].

==Biography==
When Hulas heard of [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] exploits, and his battle with [[Calo Nord]], Revan was given a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] which told him to meet Hulas on Manaan alone. Once there he tried to persuade him to hunt down [[Zuulan Sentar]] the [[Gran/Legends|Gran]], [[Lorgal]] the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]], [[Vorn Daasraad]] the [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]], [[Rulan Prolik]] the [[Changeling/Legends|shapeshifter]], and [[Ithorak Guldar]] the [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]].

Hulas failed to mention to Revan, however, that he was one of the four leaders of the GenoHaradan, and Vorn, Rulaan and Ithorak were the remaining three. With their deaths, Hulas would become the sole ruler of a powerful organization, maybe even more powerful than the [[Exchange]], since they were known only as a myth, or a rumor by most [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]].

{{GameLS|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
Revan turned down his offer, realizing that the GenoHaradan's request was unacceptable for a servant of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]].
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
===Alternative dark side story===
In the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] story, Revan complies with Hulas' request.

At the end, when Hulas reveals himself as one of the four leaders of the GenoHaradan and now the sole ruler, there are two choices: either to allow him to leave and lead the GenoHaradan or call him out for his treachery. The latter ends with a challenge to single combat in the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]] outside the walls of [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]], on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. However, the treacherous Hulas arrives with a large group of GenoHaradan assassins including [[J'ane Khan]], even if Revan goes alone. In that choice, Hulas and his other GenoHaradan [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] are slain on the sands of Tatooine by the former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], leaving the GenoHaradan leaderless.

If Revan kills the remaining Overseers after his [[Skirmish aboard the Leviathan|encounter]] on the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], Hulas' spies would know exactly who he is. When Revan and Hulas meet again, Hulas reveals that he knew everything, but will keep it as a secret.
